title: He's Seen More UFO Evidence Than Anyone Alive (Ft. Jacques Vallee)
summary: Jacques Vallee, legendary UFO researcher and computer scientist, discusses his book 'Trinity: The Best Kept Secret' about a 1945 UFO crash near the Trinity atomic test site. The interview explores Vallee's theories on UFOs as interdimensional phenomena, control systems, the nature of reality, and the relationship between science and spirituality in UFO research.

acronyms.definition: Early version of the internet that Jacques Vallee helped build
A simulated human in a computer simulation context
Field of computer science focused on creating intelligent machines

events.description: Trinity atomic bomb test occurred in New Mexico, followed 20 days later by alleged UFO crash in San Antonio, New Mexico
Kenneth Arnold coined the term 'Flying Saucer' and Roswell incident occurred
Betty and Barney Hill abduction case occurred

key_claims.claim: A UFO crash occurred in San Antonio, New Mexico in 1945, just 20 days after the Trinity atomic test and two days after Japanese surrender

Two children, Remy Baca (age 7) and Jose Padilla (age 9), witnessed the crashed craft and saw three humanoid creatures approximately three feet tall with two eyes, two arms, three fingers, and two legs
People with Above Top Secret clearances in the Pentagon have never heard of the Trinity case because it may fall under separate Department of Energy atomic secrets clearances
Bob Lazar may have been an MK Ultra patient subject to government brainwashing
Jacques Vallee helped build ARPANET with Doug Engelbart
Philip Corso claimed to be part of the Roswell cleanup crew and that reverse engineering led to fiber optics, lasers, integrated circuits, and Kevlar
Chris Mellon was supposed to meet someone from an aerospace company involved in UFO reverse engineering activity but the person died of a heart attack two weeks before the meeting
Jacques Vallee saw a UFO outside his room when he was 15 years old
UFO phenomena use intermittent reinforcement as a control mechanism similar to behavioral psychology techniques
